opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My feet are burning and itching so badly I can't even put socks on, and the skin between my toes is raw and leaking.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It started about five days ago as a bit of redness but it's got much worse in the last day — the itch is unbearable, it burns when I take my shoes off, and the cracks between my toes are weeping.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 5 days ago, started as mild redness between the toes
symptom_narrative.duration: 5 days, but much worse in the last 24 hours
symptom_narrative.character: intense itching and burning, with raw, soggy, peeling skin and painful cracks between the toes that leak a clear fluid
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: so bad I can't put socks or shoes on and I'm walking around barefoot at home
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: mainly between the toes, especially the outer two, and the soles feel thick and dry
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, worse when I take my shoes off and expose my feet to the air
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: taking my shoes off, and it feels like the talcum powder I put on made it sting more
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can't wear shoes, restricted to walking barefoot inside the house

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: generic talcum powder (self-applied to the feet)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: uses communal showers at a local gym regularly; lives alone

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: the skin between my toes is white and soggy and peeling, worst between the last two toes; there are deep painful cracks that leak a bit of clear fluid; the soles and edges of my feet look thick, dry and scaly
a damp gym bag and sweaty sneakers by the front door; the talcum powder has clumped up in the cracks between my toes
